items.title: OSIM implementează Practicile Comune (PC16) și (PC17) începând cu 27 aprilie 2026
The Inventor is still Human: Indian Patent Office’s DABUS Refusal
items.description: In keeping with a broad global trend, the Indian Patent Office has refused Dr. Stephen Thaler’s patent application which sought to recognise Dr. Thaler’s AI system DABUS as the inventor of a ‘food container and devices and methods for attracting enhanced attention’. The Indian...

items.title: Barry v. DePuy Returns: En Banc Petition Tests EcoFactor’s Reach on Rule 702 Gatekeeping
items.description: DePuy's en banc petition in Barry v. DePuy Synthes argues the panel reintroduced the weight-not-admissibility framework that EcoFactor and the 2023 Rule 702 amendments rejected. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Strike Two for CPC at High Court in Rule 36 Challenge
items.description: The U.S. Supreme Court today denied a petition for certiorari challenging the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit’s (CAFC’s) use of Rule 36 summary affirmances in appeals from the Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB). CPC Patent Technologies petitioned the Court in...

items.title: Supreme Court Denies Cert in Rapunzel Trademark Case
items.description: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday denied a petition for a writ of certiorari filed by Rebecca Curtin, leaving in place a decision by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) that barred her from opposing the trademark registration for “RAPUNZEL” for dolls and toy...

items.title: The Supreme Court Picks the Wrong Patent Fight in Hikma v. Amarin
items.description: The Supreme Court’s decision to hear Hikma Pharmaceuticals USA Inc. v. Amarin Pharma, Inc. is puzzling—and revealing. On its face, the case presents a narrow question: whether a generic drug manufacturer can be held liable for inducing patent infringement based on how it markets...

items.title: Merz and A&O gain sales ban for generic fampridine products in Germany
items.description: Merz is fighting in Germany and at the UPC over its drug Fampyra, which aims to improve walking ability in multiple sclerosis patients. At the centre of the dispute are EP 2 377 536 and EP 1 732 548. Both cover methods of using sustained release aminopyridine compositions and...

items.title: South Africa ups verification to crack down on unsafe Chinese imports
items.description: South Africa is introducing stricter verification measures for imports from China, targeting safety concerns but potentially increasing costs for sellers on platforms like Shein and Temu.

items.title: EU General Court rejects opposition to Irish Grass Fed Beef PGI
items.description: Last week, the EU General Court dismissed an opposition to the Irish Grass Fed Beef PGI in Case T-62/24. A group called the Independent Farmers Of Ireland (IFOI) opposed the Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) on the grounds that they were deprived of procedural rights to...

items.title: Cloned pharmacy sites fuel counterfeit weight-loss jab surge, warns National Pharmacy Association
items.description: Criminals are exploiting trusted pharmacy brands and regulatory logos online, as new data reveals one-in-10 online pharmacies have been cloned in the past year.

items.title: AIR-1, Right of Publicity-0: Delhi HC’s CLATastrophic Mix-Up
items.description: This post is co-authored with Dr. Aakanksha Kumar. Dr. Aakanksha Kumar (She/Her) is an independent researcher and academic who also consults with content creators and advises Chhattisgarhi music artists on copyright-related matters. Previously, she served as Associate Professor,...

items.title: Spanish Police take down manga piracy giant in Korean-backed operation
items.description: The shutdown of popular manga and manhwa platform TuMangaOnline has been confirmed as the result of a cross-border enforcement action – and its backers say more are coming.

items.title: Another win for Abbott as Court of Appeal grants PI against Sinocare
items.description: Abbott thus wins outright at the UPC in the second instance. The company filed two PI applications with The Hague local division in June 2024. Abbott alleges Sinocare and its distribution partner Menarini infringe its EP 4 344 633 and EP 3 988 471 and seeks to halt sales of...

items.title: SpicyIP Weekly Review (April 13 – April 19)
items.description: Entering the second half of April with a post on the ANI v OpenAI, judgment of which has been reserved by the Delhi HC. Can an entity that is no longer a registered copyright society continue to demand licensing fees? Post on PPL’s ongoing litigation. Another post on the Delhi...

items.title: Neuschwansteiner – When a fairy-tale castle casts its spell on trade mark law
items.description: Can the mystique of a world-famous castle prevent its name from functioning as a trade mark? In its Neuschwansteiner decision (case 26 W (pat) 34/17), the German Patent Court ("GPC") grappled with the case law of the Court of Justice of the EU ("CJEU") on geographical...

items.title: Federal Circuit Reverses Trade Secret and Patent Invalidity Rulings in Penile Implant Dispute
items.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) issued a precedential decision Friday in International Medical Devices, Inc. v. Cornell, reversing the United States District Court for the Central District of California's denial of judgment as a matter of law (JMOL) on...

items.title: Repair, Remove, Replace: DHC saves the Repair Industry
items.description: The question of whether a refurbisher, after repairing a product, can remove the affixed mark and replace it with his own has generated a lot of discussion on this blog. Last year, in a post, I had showed how the Delhi HC went from initially injuncting further sale of repaired...

items.title: Anthropic source code leak; B2B trademark strategy; ‘pastiche’ defined; and much more
items.description: Your Sunday recap of the week’s biggest developments in trademark and brand protection.

items.title: The EU’s Voss Report: ‘A Wrong Step in the Right Direction?’
items.description: While grounded in legitimate concerns, the AI copyright recommendations recently adopted by the European Parliament are unlikely to create the desired result, argues a new opinion paper of the Innovation Law and Ethics Observatory.

items.title: Same Problem, Same Solution: Reading Trade Secrets Across Fields
items.description: Federal Circuit reverses $17M Penuma judgment, tightening California's 'generally known' trade secret rule and tying it to patent inventorship. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Other Barks & Bites for Friday, April 17: CAFC Reverses District Court Rulings in Penile Implant Patent Case; CJEU Clarifies Scope of ‘Pastiche’ Copyright Exception; Stanford AI Index Shows Tech Gap Between U.S. And China Nearly Gone
items.description: This week in Other Barks & Bites: the Federal Circuit reverses a California district court’s denial of JMOL on trade secret and patent inventorship claims related to a cosmetic penile implant; the Senate Commerce Committee unanimously advances a bill to reauthorize the National...

items.title: Champagne problems? Punk solutions Amyl and the Sniffers-style
items.description: Champagne Problem Series Image‘Hey! When you get down, oh you’re a lit one,Never been a dull one, Always been a big star’(Amyl and the Sniffers, Big Dreams)Big star indeed, Amy Louise Taylor. Musician, activist and all-round punk extraordinaire, best known as the songwriter and...

items.title: Former litigators of August Debouzy and Aramis join forces for new French IP boutique
items.description: Anaïs Pallut and Annabelle Divoy’s new firm Pairs Law will cover the full range of IP matters, including patents, trademarks, copyright, designs and software, but with a clear focus on patent litigation. Both lawyers have several years of experience in well-established patent...

items.title: Clipper Tea’s UKIPO proceedings a reminder that prior rights beat “notions of heritage”
items.description: Clipper Tea successfully proved indirect confusion against The 1872 Clipper Tea Co but could have secured an easier win with a solid word mark for any stylisation of CLIPPER, says a local expert.

items.title: Twenty One Pilots v Temu moves forward; Alt Legal acquires WebTMS – news digest
items.description: Five bite-sized IP updates from the week, also featuring rising auto industry concern over IP theft and Temu joining the IACC.

items.title: Understanding GI filing trends in India
items.description: An analysis of registry data reveals a rise in domestic GI filings in recent years, alongside persistently high pendency levels and limited, declining international participation.

items.title: Extra Credit Not Required: Teva v. Lilly and the Limits of Amgen’s Reach
items.description: Federal Circuit reverses JMOL in Teva v. Lilly, holding that method-of-use claims face a lower Section 112 bar when the genus is well known and not itself the invention. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: USPTO Pushes for More ASAP! Search Pilot Participants with Extended Deadline
items.description: The U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (UPSTO) announced today that it will be extending the Artificial Intelligence Search Automated Pilot Program (ASAP!) until June 1, 2026, to gather additional information and continue evaluating the program's effectiveness. The Pilot was first...

items.title: Federal Circuit Distinguishes Amgen in Reversal of Invalidation of Teva Headache Treatment Patents
items.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) issued a precedential decision today in Teva Pharmaceuticals International GmbH v. Eli Lilly and Company, reversing the United States District Court for the District of Massachusetts’s grant of judgment as a matter of law...

items.title: Corning and Quinn Emanuel defend special glass production method against TCL
items.description: The injunction refers exclusively to Germany. Although the patent is still valid in France, Corning had apparently decided, for strategic reasons, not to pursue infringement proceedings there. Corning, a major US technology company specialising in advanced glass and materials...

items.title: WIPO warning signals dangerous new phase in email fraud targeting trademark users
items.description: According to updates from WIPO and the EUIPO, scams targeting IP applicants are becoming more sophisticated, more convincing and more financially ambitious.

items.title: Schedule A Litigation: Strategies and Best Practices for Online Enforcement
items.description: In 2025, trademark cases filed in United States District Courts increased 25% from 2024 (up 848 cases to 4,211). Many of those cases were “Schedule A” lawsuits, a niche form of intellectual property litigation that joins multiple foreign-based ecommerce stores selling...

items.title: How to put trademarks at the heart of B2B trust building
items.description: Trademark counsel at food preservative company Corbion explains how embedding IP early, upstream and visibly into business decisions turns trademarks from reactive legal assets into a core tool for risk management and trust building.

items.title: Never Too Late: If you missed the IPKat last week!
items.description: It was a lively week on the IPKat, with a set of posts full of insights worth your attention. In case you didn’t catch everything, here’s your round-up: Fordham IP Conference Kat taking a short nap between conference sessionsKatfriends Sarah Bui and Seth B. Pearson took us to...

items.title: Consumer, Patient Groups Push for Fast Markup of ETHIC Act
items.description: A number of groups and individuals self-described as “representing consumers, patients, health care providers, and academic experts in pharmaceutical policy and patent law” have submitted a letter to the U.S. House Judiciary Committee urging members to quickly advance the...

items.title: Extraordinary by Design: How the USPTO Is Bypassing Its Own Reexamination Rules
items.description: USPTO's new pre-order procedure lets patent owners oppose ex parte reexamination before the SNQ determination, but the legal basis is shaky. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Regional Court Munich puts decision in ZTE vs Samsung on hold
items.description: The current case is one of eight actions — four at the UPC and four at the Regional Court Munich — in which ZTE and Samsung are going head to head over SEPs for 4G and 5G technology. Each party has sued the other over four of their own patents. Today’s case concerns ZTE’s EP […]...

items.title: CAFC Affirms District Court Invalidation of Controller Patent
items.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) issued a precedential decision Tuesday in Definitive Holdings, LLC v. Powerteq LLC, affirming the United States District Court for the District of Utah’s grant of summary judgment of invalidity of Definitive Holdings'...

items.title: ANI v OpenAI: Not Everything an LLM Does is Copyright Infringement
items.description: With judgment now reserved in ANI v OpenAI, India stands at the cusp of what might be its first major judicial reckoning with the copyright implications of generative AI. The case raises foundational questions on whether AI systems merely process information in new ways or...

items.title: DHC Stunts the Growth of Dynamic Injunctions, Demands the Legislature to Step In
items.description: On 16 March 2026, a single bench (SB) of the Delhi High Court (DHC), in Mahindra and Mahindra Limited v. Diksha Sharma, provided a decisive inflection point in the evolution of dynamic injunction jurisprudence in India. The case started from a fairly typical trademark dispute...

items.title: AI in the patent industry: Don't believe the hype. Believe the data.
items.description: Many in the IP profession remain considerably sceptical of AI. AI may be useful for checking for typos and simple calculations of deadlines, but it cannot replace in-depth human reasoning about complex scientific and legal issues. However, the data suggests something different....

items.title: CJEU delivers pastiche-style judgment on … pastiche
items.description: Moses PelhamToday, the Grand Chamber of the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) issued its judgment in Pelham II, C-590/23, concerning the notion of pastiche in Article 5(3)(k) of the InfoSoc Directive.To understand the genesis of the referral, reference must be made...

items.title: Fordham 33 (Report 5): Trade secrets
items.description: The Fordham trade secrets panel discussingthe rise of trade secrets protection The IPKat is back in Fordham, with the Spring skies and the chirping birds gracing the streets of the Upper West Side. As a proud and long-standing partner with the Fordham IP Conference, now in its...

items.title: Fordham 33 (Report 4): Life sciences and healthcare innovation
items.description: An invigorating sprint through globallife sciences issuesThe IPKat is back in Fordham, with the Spring skies and the chirping birds gracing the streets of the Upper West Side. As a proud and long-standing partner with the Fordham IP Conference, now in its 33rd year, the IPKat is...

items.title: Mind the Gap: The Middle Layer of Obviousness Doctrine
items.description: Graham's four factors organize the evidence but don't resolve obviousness. The doctrines built atop them do the real analytical work. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Fisher & Paykel and WBH successful in revoking Flexicare’s patent in Milan
items.description: At the centre of the revocation claim filed by Fisher & Paykel is Flexicare’s EP 4 185 356 relating to a nasal cannula designed to improve patient comfort and usability during high-flow oxygen therapy. The nasal cannula comprises a manifold, non-sealing nasal prongs and a...

items.title: All UPC judges and their current capacities
items.description: The roster of judges at the UPC is steadily growing. While there were 124 legally and technically qualified judges in mid-2025, by the end of the year that number had already risen to 129. By March 2026, the number of judges had increased by another five as Thomas Adocker, Goda...

items.title: Sequence identities and functional definitions - where is the limit? (T 0137/24)
items.description: Biotechnology patents often rely on defining protein and nucleic acid features by their sequence identity. However, it is often not necessary to define the exact sequence, but instead to define the sequence according to a limit of sequence identity, e.g. a sequence having a 90%...

items.title: [Guest post] Oh my influencer! Italian rules on influencer marketing
items.description: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest contribution by Katfriend Maria Cristina Michelini (Baglioni Hotels & Resorts) detailing developments in the Italian regulatory system for influencers. Here’s what she writes:Oh my influencer! Italian rules on...

items.title: From Delhi to Bombay, Music Licensing Goes Off the Beat
items.description: A lot has been happening with the repertoire of sound recordings that Phonographic Performance Limited (PPL) claims to own and manage. It is being used in saree showrooms, a group of 94 restaurants, high profile pubs and bars, restaurants and more. Against all these places, PPL...

items.title: On the way to a global FRAND rate: Nokia drops claims against Warner and Paramount
items.description: According to the UPC register, the proceedings concerning EP 4 250 732, over which Nokia had sued Warner Bros. and Paramount in autumn 2025, were withdrawn before Easter (case IDs: UPC_CFI_975/2025 and UPC_CFI_739/2026 and UPC_CFI_1390/2025). In response to an enquiry by JUVE...

items.title: SpicyIP Bells & Whistles: IP Events and Opportunities (13.04.2026)
items.description: Welcome back to another week of Bells & Whistles. As always, we’ve rounded up a mix of developments, opportunities, and thoughtful reads from across the IP world along with a Bell of the Week that’s well worth revisiting. Bell of the Week: Médecins Sans Frontières Some bells do...

items.title: PTAB Inventory Drops Below 2,000 for the First Time in 20 Years
items.description: PTAB inventory drops below 2,000 for the first time as appeal pendency falls to 9 months and IPR institutions decline 43% under Dir. Squires. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: SpicyIP Weekly Review (April 6 – April 12)
items.description: Entering the second week of April announcing the faculty line up for the SpicyIP Summer School 2026! Two-part post on the purpose of copyright in academic work in the context of Sci-Hub litigation. Another post discussing whether trademark law can be used to reclaim what design...

items.title: Win without gain: Microsoft prevails in cost dispute with Suinno
items.description: Finnish company Suinno is the owner of EP 2 671 173 covering a “method and means for browsing by walking”. It relates to people browsing the internet by walking to discover search results as they come into close proximity. The Helsinki-based company accuses Microsoft of...

items.title: Marked for Trouble: Settlement Licenses and the § 287 Trap for NPEs
items.description: Federal Circuit panel hammers NPE counsel on whether settlement licenses trigger the § 287 marking requirement for pre-suit patent damages. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: The Dark Matter of Patent Law: Nearly 25% of Office Actions Now Cite Secret Prior Art
items.description: Empirical study of 233M citations shows secret prior art is growing, but the legal measure overstates the practical problem by about a quarter. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: New patent owner Silimed lands PI against Polytech at Munich court
items.description: Silimed has achieved its first success in the infringement dispute over breast implant technology. After the Brazilian manufacturer secured the rights to a patent for the manufacture of breast implants in a complex entitlement action, Silimed is now taking measures against its...

items.title: The Symmetry Problem: Printed Matter, Nexus, and the Federal Circuit’s One-Way Ratchet
items.description: Federal Circuit hears EagleView v. Nearmap on whether printed matter doctrine can be used to defeat secondary considerations nexus showing. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: X Marks the … Stick Figure? Federal Circuit Says One DuPont Factor Can Outweigh All Others
items.description: Federal Circuit affirms no likelihood of confusion between cigar maker's X marks and vape company's stick figure design, holding mark dissimilarity alone can be dispositive. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: (Part II) Right Without Duty: Academic Copyright, the Publisher’s Juridical Fiction, and the Case for an Ex Ante Reading of Section 52
items.description: Continuing the discussion on the Scihub litigation, in Part II of their post, Rishabh Upadhyay and Pragati Upadhyay turn to the August 2025 order as a vantage point to examine what the litigation has, so far, failed to do. They argue that the procedural exclusion of intervenors...

items.title: WIPO Launches New Award for Cities of Innovation
items.description: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) today launched the “WIPO City of Innovation” award program, a new initiative that recognizes cities worldwide demonstrating exceptional commitment to fostering innovation and creativity among their residents.

items.title: WIPO Launches Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange
items.description: WIPO today launched the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange (AIII), a new initiative for expert dialogue on IP and AI issues that will focus on technical and operational issues and complement ongoing discussions at other WIPO forums.

items.title: Albania is First to Join WIPO’s Riyadh Design Law Treaty
items.description: Albania today became the first member state to join WIPO’s Riyadh Design Law Treaty (RDLT), depositing its instrument of ratification with Director General Daren Tang and marking a major milestone in bringing into force the new international framework for design protection.

items.title: International Patent Applications Rose in 2025, with Digital Communication and Semiconductor Technologies Showing Strong Growth
items.description: International patent applications filed through WIPO's Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) rose 0.7% in 2025 to reach 275,900 filings worldwide, marking a second consecutive year of growth. The increase was partly due to robust activity in ICT related fields, with digital...
